name: Zarathushtra
shortDescription: Zarathushtra, also known as Zoroaster, was an ancient Iranian prophet and founder of Zoroastrianism. He is revered for his teachings on monotheism, good versus evil, and the importance of moral choices.
description: Zarathushtra, the founder of Zoroastrianism, is a pivotal figure in Persian mythology and religious history. Born in ancient Persia (likely in the 6th century BCE, though dates vary), he experienced a profound spiritual awakening around the age of thirty. This awakening led him to believe in Ahura Mazda, the supreme creator and benevolent god, and to reject the polytheistic traditions of his time. Zarathushtra preached against animal sacrifice and promoted the concepts of good thoughts, good words, and good deeds as the path to righteousness. His teachings emphasize the cosmic struggle between Ahura Mazda and Angra Mainyu, the embodiment of evil, and the eventual triumph of good. He spent years wandering, facing opposition and persecution, before finding patronage with King Vishtaspa, who converted to Zoroastrianism and helped spread the faith. Zarathushtra's legacy lies in his revolutionary religious and ethical system, which influenced later monotheistic religions and continues to be practiced by Zoroastrians worldwide. He is considered a prophet and a wise teacher who illuminated the path to spiritual enlightenment and moral responsibility.
quests: Receiving the revelation from Ahura Mazda

sources.title: The Religion of the Good Religion
sources.author: Werner Sundermann
